team_context.cap_situation: flush — ~$85M in cap space entering 2026 free agency (4th-most in NFL), $301.2M salary cap. Have already spent on Biadasz, Mack, Tart, Strange, Penning, Kolar, Ingold. Still have ample room remaining.
team_context.trade_posture: Buyers who are aggressive about building through the draft (Baltimore DNA from Hortiz). Went 11-6 back-to-back years, made playoffs both seasons under Harbaugh. Lost in Wild Card both times. New OC Mike McDaniel replacing Greg Roman. New DC Chris O'Leary replacing Jesse Minter (now Ravens HC). Team urgently wants to push past Wild Card round. OL was historically bad in 2025 (Herbert sacked 54 times). Both Slater and Alt returning from season-ending injuries. Tuli Tuipulotu extension-eligible and a top priority. Will be buyers at OL and EDGE depth.

players.name: Justin Herbert
players.performance_evidence: 2x Pro Bowler (2021, 2025). In 2025: 3,727 passing yards, 26 TD, career-high 498 rushing yards behind the worst OL in NFL. Led team to 11 wins despite being sacked 54 times (2nd-most in NFL). PFF grade 85.7 at midseason — best on the team. In 2024: 3,870 yards, 23 TD, 3 INT (8th-best TD:INT ratio in NFL history for 15+ TD seasons). Franchise QB in his prime.

trade_value.reasoning: Franchise QB in his prime on a long-term deal. Essentially untradeable — the $72.8M dead cap alone makes it financially impossible, and he is the franchise. Worth multiple 1sts in theory but will never be moved.

players.availability_reasoning: Face of the franchise, entering his prime at 28, signed through 2029. $72.8M dead cap makes a trade financially catastrophic. Zero chance under any circumstance.
players.injury_notes: Fractured left (non-throwing) hand in Week 13 of 2025, underwent surgery, played through it for remainder of season. Expected fully healthy for 2026.

players.name: DJ Uiagalelei
players.performance_evidence: Spent entire 2025 season on practice squad. Elevated for spot duty. No meaningful NFL stats. Chargers may still bring back Trey Lance or add another backup QB.

trade_value.reasoning: Practice squad-level backup QB with no trade value. Futures contract player.

players.availability_reasoning: Minimum-salary backup with no established role. Would be released before traded. Essentially no trade market.

players.name: Omarion Hampton
players.performance_evidence: Rookie in 2025: 124 carries, 545 yards, 4 TD in 9 games (4.4 YPC). Missed significant time with ankle injury (IR from Week 5). Bell-cow usage when healthy — averaged 17.3 touches/game with 79%+ snap share in 4 full games. PFF grade 73.0. New OC Mike McDaniel's scheme should unlock more of his talent in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: 1st-round RB on a cheap rookie deal with 4 years of control plus 5th-year option. Showed flashes as a rookie but missed time with injury. The positional devaluation of RB limits his trade value, but the draft capital invested and contract control keep it in the late 2nd range.

players.availability_reasoning: 2025 first-round pick, centerpiece of the run game going forward. Mike McDaniel specifically cited Hampton as a player he targeted in the draft. Zero chance the Chargers move him after one year.
players.injury_notes: Ankle injury in Week 5 of 2025, placed on IR. Returned for Wild Card game. Expected fully healthy for 2026.

players.name: Kimani Vidal
players.performance_evidence: Career year in 2025: 643 rushing yards, 3 TD, three 100-yard games. PFF grade 73.5 at midseason. Exclusive rights tender for 2026, so essentially free for the Chargers to retain. Solid complementary back.

trade_value.reasoning: Productive change-of-pace back on a minimum deal, but RB2s on expiring deals have minimal trade value. Worth a late-round pick swap at most.

players.availability_reasoning: ERFA tendered, so the Chargers have him for cheap. Would move him for a Day 3 pick if a team wanted a quality backup RB.

players.name: Ladd McConkey
players.performance_evidence: Stellar rookie in 2024: 82 catches, 1,149 yards, 7 TD (broke Chargers rookie records). 2025 was a step back in a run-heavy offense: 66 rec, 789 yards, 6 TD. PFF grade 67.6 in 2025 (49th/81 WRs). Still just 24 with elite speed (4.39) and rapport with Herbert. New OC McDaniel expected to feature him more — compared to Jaxon Smith-Njigba's leap. NFL Top 100 player.

trade_value.reasoning: Young WR1 on a team-friendly 2nd-round rookie contract with 3 years of control. Elite ceiling shown as a rookie (1,149 yards). 2025 dip was scheme-related (lowest pass volume in NFL under Greg Roman). With McDaniel installing a new scheme, expect a bounceback. Worth a high 2nd-round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Herbert's top target, on a bargain 2nd-round rookie deal, entering Year 3 with new pass-friendly OC Mike McDaniel. The Chargers view McConkey as a core offensive building block.
players.injury_notes: Minor foot issue late in 2025, did not miss games.

players.name: Quentin Johnston
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025 season: 51 rec, 735 yards, 8 TD in 14 games (14.4 YPR). Was WR4 in PPR through first 4 weeks. PFF grade 67.6. Inconsistent — 28 catches for 407 yards and 5 TD in first 6 games, then slumped before a late resurgence (9 catches, 202 yards, 1 TD in final 2 games). Big-play ability (104-yard game vs Cowboys). Still has 5th-year option available in 2027.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-round pick who finally showed his potential in Year 3 with 8 TDs. Still on a cheap rookie deal with a 5th-year option. Worth a late 2nd/early 3rd to a team that believes in his breakout trajectory.

players.availability_reasoning: The Chargers invested 1st-round capital and have seen the flashes. With the new McDaniel offense, they likely want to see what he can do in Year 4. But if someone offered a 2nd, the front office would have to strongly consider it given his inconsistency.
players.injury_notes: Groin issue limited practice in December 2025. Generally healthy.

players.name: Tre' Harris
players.performance_evidence: Rookie in 2025: 30 rec, 324 yards, 1 TD. Was the WR4 in a crowded room with McConkey, Johnston, and Keenan Allen ahead of him. Made key plays when targeted (12 of 23 receptions went for first downs, zero drops). PFF noted his blocking was impressive. With Allen likely gone, Harris moves into WR3 role in 2026.

trade_value.reasoning: 2nd-round rookie on a cheap deal with 4 years of control. Limited production so far but talent was evident at Ole Miss (1,030 yards in 8 games). Expected bigger role in 2026 with Keenan Allen gone. Worth a 4th-round pick based on upside and contract.

players.availability_reasoning: Chargers drafted him to be the X receiver of the future alongside McConkey. With Allen gone, his role expands significantly. Wouldn't move him cheaply but could be had for the right offer.
players.injury_notes: Jaw issue limited practice in November 2025. Hip issue. Nothing serious.

players.name: Oronde Gadsden
players.performance_evidence: Solid rookie season: 49 catches, 664 yards, 3 TD. PFF grade 84.0 in Week 3 debut vs Broncos. Developed quick rapport with Herbert during offseason. Athletic pass-catching TE. The Chargers may still draft TE Kenyon Sadiq in Round 1 to complement him.

trade_value.reasoning: Productive rookie TE on a cheap 3rd-round contract with 3 years of control. Not elite, but a reliable target who showed immediate chemistry with Herbert. Worth a mid-to-late 3rd round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: The Chargers like Gadsden but beat reporters note they could still draft TE Kenyon Sadiq at pick 22. If they draft another TE, Gadsden's value would adjust but he'd still be a core piece. Not being shopped.

players.name: Charlie Kolar
players.performance_evidence: Signed as highest-paid blocking TE in NFL ($24.3M/3yr). Was with Ravens (2022-2025). Reunites with Hortiz. At 6-6, 265 lbs, he's a mauler at the point of attack — perfect fit for McDaniel's scheme. Limited receiving production in career but that's not his role.

trade_value.reasoning: Blocking TE on a relatively expensive deal for the role. Limited trade market — teams don't trade for blocking TEs. Worth a 4th/5th at best to a run-heavy team.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed so not going anywhere, but the contract structure is such that he could be moved in-season if things don't work out with minimal dead cap impact.

players.name: Rashawn Slater
players.performance_evidence: 2x Pro Bowler, 2nd-team All-Pro (rookie year). PFF 90.9 overall grade in 2024 (2nd among all tackles), 88.3 pass-blocking grade over 3 healthy seasons (3rd among OTs). Signed 4-year, $114M extension in July 2025 — highest-paid OL in NFL history at the time. Anchors Herbert's blindside.

trade_value.reasoning: Elite LT in his prime on a long-term deal. One of the top 3-5 tackles in football. Worth multiple 1sts in theory. But the massive dead cap ($52M) makes a trade financially impossible.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a record-setting extension in July 2025. $52M dead cap. Cornerstone of the offense. Absolutely untouchable.
players.injury_notes: Suffered season-ending knee injury during 2025 training camp, missed entire 2025 season. Also missed 2022 with torn biceps (returned strong). Expected healthy for 2026.

players.name: Trevor Penning
players.performance_evidence: Former 1st-round pick (Saints, 2022). Acquired via trade deadline 2025 for a 2027 6th-rounder. Started 4 of 7 games with LAC, mostly as extra OL/6th man. Started all 17 games in 2024 with Saints. Versatile — can play both tackle and guard. Re-signed on 1-year, $4.5M deal.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-rounder who has never fully lived up to draft pedigree. Versatile swing lineman on a 1-year deal. Worth a 5th/6th round pick as a depth piece.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed but on a cheap 1-year deal. If the Chargers draft an OL early or sign another guard, Penning becomes depth and could be moved for a Day 3 pick.

players.name: Tyler Biadasz
players.performance_evidence: Former Pro Bowler (2022 with Cowboys). PFF overall grade of 72.0 since 2021 ranks 7th among centers with 3,500+ snaps. Started 31 games for Commanders over last 2 seasons. Ranked 15th in pass block win rate and 16th in run block win rate in 2025. Consistent, durable — has started 15+ games each of past 5 seasons.

trade_value.reasoning: Reliable starting center on a fair 3-year deal. Not elite but a clear upgrade over Bozeman. Worth a mid-3rd round pick given contract control and positional scarcity.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed to address their biggest need. Won't be traded unless they draft a center, which is unlikely given they just invested $30M. Premium price if someone comes calling.

players.name: Cole Strange
players.performance_evidence: Former 1st-round pick (Patriots, 2022). Started 14 games at RG for Dolphins in 2025 under Mike McDaniel. PFF credited him with 21 pressures allowed. Ranked 26th among guards in PBWR (91.9%), 59th in RBWR (65.8%). Missed most of 2023 with torn patellar tendon. Reunites with McDaniel in LAC.

trade_value.reasoning: Former 1st-rounder who has been inconsistent but has scheme familiarity with McDaniel. Concerns about being a long-term starter. On a 2-year, $13M deal. Worth a 4th/5th to a team needing OL depth.

players.availability_reasoning: Signed to compete for starting RG job, but if the Chargers draft a guard in Round 1 or 2, Strange becomes expensive depth and could be moved.
players.injury_notes: Torn patellar tendon in 2023 that cost most of the season. Has been healthy since.

players.name: Joe Alt
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowler in 2025 (before injury). PFF 79.0 overall grade in 2025 (17th/89 tackles), 82.3 pass-blocking grade (6th/89). 91.4 pass-blocking grade in Week 1 at LT. As a 2024 rookie, led all rookie tackles in PFF grade (75.9), was 8th-best RT in NFL by season's end. Named PFWA All-Rookie Team. Only 23 — ascending rapidly. Has 5th-year option available.

trade_value.reasoning: Franchise RT/LT who is 23 years old, ascending, on a rookie deal with 3 years + 5th-year option. Already a Pro Bowler. Young, elite OT prospects are worth multiple 1sts. One of the most valuable non-QB trade assets in the NFL.

players.availability_reasoning: 5th overall pick in 2024, already a Pro Bowler at 22. Generational OT talent on a rookie deal. Extension coming. The Chargers would never trade him — period.
players.injury_notes: Suffered season-ending ankle injury midway through 2025 (after 6 games). Expected fully healthy for 2026.

players.name: Scott Matlock
players.performance_evidence: 7th-round pick who has carved out a role as a FB/DL hybrid. Pro Bowl alternate at FB in 2025. Versatile, tough, beloved by coaching staff. Limited stats as a defensive lineman but plays special teams and short-yardage role.

trade_value.reasoning: Versatile role player on a minimum deal. His value is mostly as a culture/special teams piece. Worth a 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap depth piece. With Alec Ingold signed as the new FB, Matlock's FB role is diminished. Could be moved if someone wants a versatile big man.

players.name: Teair Tart
players.performance_evidence: Signed 3-year, $30M extension ($20M guaranteed) in Jan 2026. In 2025: 32 tackles, 4 TFL, forced fumble, fumble recovery in all 18 games. PFF run defense grade 74.7 (5th among IDL with 240+ snaps). PFF overall 67.9. Highest-graded defender on team at midseason (83.2 PFF run defense). 12 run stops ranked 7th in NFL.

trade_value.reasoning: Above-average run-stuffing DT on a fair 3-year deal just signed. The Chargers value him highly as the anchor of their run defense. Worth a mid-3rd but just signed a new deal, so not moving.

players.availability_reasoning: Just signed a 3-year extension in January 2026. Not going anywhere. Would only move for a significant overpay.

players.name: Justin Eboigbe
players.performance_evidence: 6th-round pick in 2023. Rotational DL, primarily a run defender. Limited stats. Depth piece on a cheap deal entering final year.

trade_value.reasoning: Rotational DL depth on a minimum deal. Minimal trade value — worth a conditional 7th at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Cheap depth player on an expiring deal. Available for any reasonable offer.

players.name: Tuli Tuipulotu
players.performance_evidence: Breakout 2025: 13 sacks (tied for 3rd in NFL), 20 TFL, 23 QB hits, 2 forced fumbles, 69 pressures (8th among EDGE). First Pro Bowl selection. In 2024: 8.5 sacks. Career 26 sacks in 3 seasons. At just 23, one of the highest-ceiling pass rushers in football. Extension talks haven't started yet but Hortiz said 'we want Tuli around for a long time.'

trade_value.reasoning: 23-year-old Pro Bowl EDGE on the final year of his rookie deal. Elite production (13 sacks) at a premium position with his entire prime ahead of him. If traded, worth multiple 1sts — comparable to what elite young pass rushers command. The Chargers will extend him; this is purely hypothetical value.

players.availability_reasoning: Chargers' most important defensive player, entering the final year of his rookie deal. Extension is the team's #1 offseason priority. GM Hortiz explicitly said they want to keep him long-term. Zero chance of trading him.
players.trade_buzz: Extension-eligible this offseason. Projected to command $100-150M. Hortiz said talks haven't started but 'we want Tuli around for a long time.'

players.name: Daiyan Henley
players.performance_evidence: Team captain in 2025. Starting ILB for 2 seasons. PFF grade 49.7 at midseason — hurt by poor coverage grades (42.7). Has leadership value but is a below-average starter. Chargers have not invested heavily in LB.

trade_value.reasoning: Starting ILB on an expiring rookie deal. Leadership value but poor PFF grades, especially in coverage. Worth a 5th/6th round pick.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of rookie deal. The Chargers value his leadership but might not re-sign him at market rate. Available for fair value.

players.name: Troy Dye
players.performance_evidence: PFF grade 82.9 in one standout game vs Broncos but generally a rotational LB. Under contract for 2026. Depth piece in the LB room.

trade_value.reasoning: Depth LB on a cheap deal. Minimal trade value. Worth a late-round pick to a LB-needy team at most.

players.availability_reasoning: Rotational depth. Available for any reasonable offer.

players.name: Khalil Mack
players.performance_evidence: Future Hall of Famer, 9x Pro Bowler, 2016 DPOY. In 2025: 5.5 sacks, 23 pressures in 12 games — production clearly declining from 17 sacks in 2023. Missed 4 games with elbow injury. At 35, no longer an impact pass rusher on a down-to-down basis. Still immensely valuable as a leader and mentor (credited with Tuipulotu's development). 113 career sacks (28th all-time).

trade_value.reasoning: Declining 35-year-old on a fully guaranteed $18M deal. Production has dropped significantly but leadership and locker room value are exceptional. A contender might give a 4th for a half-season rental, but the full guarantee and age cap his trade market.

players.availability_reasoning: Just re-signed on a 1-year, $18M fully guaranteed deal. The Chargers brought him back specifically for leadership and continuity. Would only move him at the trade deadline if the team was out of contention, which is unlikely.
players.injury_notes: Elbow injury in 2025 caused 4-game absence. Generally durable during Chargers tenure (61 of 62 games started over 4 years).

players.name: Donte Jackson
players.performance_evidence: Excellent 2025 season: started all 17 games, 4 INTs (team-leading), career-best 12 passes defensed. PFF 73.4 coverage grade, 14.9% forced incompletion rate (73rd percentile). PFF overall held up by coverage grade of 75.0. Bad tackling (28.9 PFF tackling grade). 23 career INTs. His $13M/2yr deal looks like a bargain.

trade_value.reasoning: Quality starting CB1 on an affordable expiring deal. 4 INTs and 12 PDs in 2025 at $6.5M AAV is great value. Worth a mid-3rd to a CB-needy contender at the trade deadline.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year of his 2-year deal. Good starter but the Chargers have invested heavily in young CBs (Hart, Still). If a contender offers a 3rd-round pick, especially at the deadline, the Chargers would likely accept given their young CB depth.

players.name: Cam Hart
players.performance_evidence: Impressive in 2025: 37 solo tackles, 1 INT, 11 PDs. Started regularly as CB2 after being a rotational piece as a rookie. Big (6-2, 207 lbs), physical, high football IQ. PFF ranked him in top 10 among rookie CBs in passer rating against (2024). Derwin James said both Hart and Still are 'Pro Bowl corners.' 5th-round steal.

trade_value.reasoning: Young, ascending CB on a dirt-cheap 5th-round contract with 2 years left. Size-speed combo (6-2, 4.50) is coveted. Worth a 3rd-round pick given contract value and upside.

players.availability_reasoning: Chargers view Hart as a core piece of the secondary going forward. Derwin James publicly praised him as a future Pro Bowler. Only available at a clear overpay.
players.injury_notes: Torn labrum shoulder surgery after 2024 playoffs. Recovered for 2025 camp. Durability is a slight concern.

players.name: Tarheeb Still
players.performance_evidence: Led Chargers in INTs as a rookie (2024). PFF 73.7 defense grade and 73.3 coverage grade — top 5 among rookie CBs with 300+ snaps in 2024. Continued to grow in 2025 as the primary nickel corner. Khalil Mack credited Still's offseason work ethic for his growth. Another 5th-round steal.

trade_value.reasoning: Young nickel CB on a bargain 5th-round contract with 2 years left. Already starting. Excellent coverage grades for a young player. Worth a mid-3rd given age, contract, and production.

players.availability_reasoning: Core piece of the secondary. Same reasoning as Hart — the Chargers are building their CB room around these two young players. Only available at a significant overpay.

players.name: Derwin James Jr.
players.performance_evidence: Pro Bowler and 2nd-team All-Pro in 2025. In 2025: 94 tackles, 2 sacks, 3 INTs, 8 QB hits. PFF pass rush grade 78.1, coverage grade 77.2 (both strong). PFF tackling grade was poor (47.8). Versatile safety who plays in the box, covers, and rushes the passer. Still an impact player at 29 but entering final year of deal. Spotrac notes an extension should lower his $24.6M cap figure.

trade_value.reasoning: Pro Bowl safety still playing at a high level, but entering the final year of his deal with a $24.6M cap hit. Acquiring team would get one year of All-Pro production but at a premium price. Worth a late 1st/early 2nd to a contender. However, an extension could change the calculus significantly.

players.availability_reasoning: Entering final year with a massive $24.6M cap hit. Chargers want to extend him but no deal is done yet. If extension talks stall, he becomes a trade candidate — especially given the team's need to fund Tuipulotu's extension. But he's a team captain and face of the defense, so the price would be steep.
players.injury_notes: Missed 2019 and most of 2020 with injuries. Has been durable since (started 61+ games in 4 Chargers seasons). History of fines for illegal hits.
players.character_notes: Suspended 1 game in 2024 for repeated health & safety violations. Multiple fines for hits on defenseless receivers. On-field aggression is a known factor.
players.trade_buzz: Spotrac notes an extension should lower his $24.6M cap figure. Hortiz has mentioned wanting to retain him.

players.name: Elijah Molden
players.performance_evidence: Acquired via trade from Titans (cost a 2027 7th). PFF 55.2 overall grade at midseason — worst coverage grade (51.8) among the starting secondary. Below-average starter at FS. The Chargers may look to upgrade this spot.

trade_value.reasoning: Below-average starting FS on an expiring deal. Limited trade market. Worth a late-round pick at best.

players.availability_reasoning: Worst PFF grades in the secondary. Entering final year of deal. If the Chargers sign or draft a FS, Molden becomes expendable. Would accept a 6th or 7th for him.
players.injury_notes: Hamstring issue in 2025 caused missed practice time.

players.name: Trey Lance
players.performance_evidence: Former #3 overall pick who never developed into a starter. Started only 4 games for San Francisco before being traded to Dallas in 2023, where he served as a backup. Signed with the Chargers on a modest 1-year deal in 2025 free agency as a developmental backup behind Justin Herbert. Limited NFL production: career ~60% completion rate, more interceptions than touchdowns in limited action, below-average PFF grades when he has played. Still possesses impressive physical tools (arm strength, mobility) but has not been able to translate them into consistent NFL-level quarterbacking. Essentially a high-upside backup/reclamation project at this stage.

trade_value.reasoning: Former top-3 pick still generates some curiosity value due to physical tools and age (only 26), but expiring contract, near-zero starting resume, and multiple failed opportunities severely limit value. Worth a late 6th/early 7th at best to a team wanting a camp arm with upside.

players.availability_reasoning: Clear backup behind Justin Herbert with zero path to starting in LA. On a cheap expiring deal with no dead cap. Chargers would move him for any reasonable draft pick compensation, especially if a QB-needy team wants to take a flier on his tools.
players.injury_notes: Suffered a severe ankle fracture in Week 2 of 2022 that cost him the rest of the season and significantly derailed his development trajectory. Has been healthy since but the lost development time is a key factor in his limited progression.
players.trade_buzz: No significant recent trade buzz. Some speculation that QB-needy teams may call about him if a starter goes down during the season, but nothing concrete.

players.name: Dalvin Tomlinson
players.performance_evidence: Tomlinson is a veteran run-stuffing interior defensive lineman who has been a dependable starter throughout his career with the Giants, Vikings, and now the Browns/Chargers. He was a Pro Bowl selection in 2022 with Minnesota and has consistently graded as an above-average run defender by PFF. In 2024 his production dipped modestly — still effective against the run but generating minimal pass rush (1-2 sacks). He's a two-gapping, space-eating nose tackle type who anchors well and controls gaps. Not a penetrator or pass-rush threat but does his job at a solid level. At 32, he's on the downslope but still functional.

trade_value.reasoning: Aging run-stuffing DT on a modest contract still has value as a plug-and-play starter, but 32-year-old interior defenders with limited pass-rush upside don't generate much trade capital. Worth roughly a 5th-round pick. The low positional value and age curve compress his value significantly despite solid play.

players.availability_reasoning: Veteran on the back end of his career signed to provide depth and run defense for the Chargers' defensive front. With only $3.5M in dead cap if traded, the move is financially painless. If the Chargers fall out of contention or want to create playing time for younger interior linemen, Tomlinson is a logical trade candidate at the deadline.
players.injury_notes: Dealt with a calf injury in 2023 that caused him to miss several games. Generally durable throughout career but typical wear for a 32-year-old interior lineman.
